result.markdown:
---
title: function.so.wingwt
---



```python
function.so.wingwt(X)
```

Aircraft Wing Weight function.

The example models the weight of an unpainted light aircraft wing.
The function accepts inputs in the unit cube [0,1]^9 and returns the wing weight.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type                              | Description                                                                                                                                          | Default    |
|--------|-----------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| X      | [array](`array`) - [like](`like`) | Input points with shape (n_samples, 9) or (9,) or (10,) or (n_samples, 10). Input variables order: [Sw, Wfw, A, L, q, l, Rtc, Nz, Wdg, Wp(optional)] | _required_ |

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                     | Description                                                                 |
|--------|------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [np](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`) | np.ndarray: Wing weight values at the input points with shape (n_samples,). |

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

Single point evaluation (Baseline Cessna C172 - Unpainted):


::: {#b1ada5ee .cell execution_count=1}
``` {.python .cell-code}
from spotoptim.function.so import wingwt
import numpy as np
# Baseline configuration in unit cube
x_base = np.array([0.48, 0.4, 0.38, 0.5, 0.62, 0.344, 0.4, 0.37, 0.38])
wingwt(x_base)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
```
array([233.90840542])
```
:::
:::


Batch evaluation:

::: {#cc8b8dfa .cell execution_count=2}
``` {.python .cell-code}
from spotoptim.function.so import wingwt
import numpy as np
x_base = np.array([0.48, 0.4, 0.38, 0.5, 0.62, 0.344, 0.4, 0.37, 0.38])
X = np.vstack([x_base, x_base])
wingwt(X)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2}
```
array([233.90840542, 233.90840542])
```
:::
:::


## References {.doc-section .doc-section-references}

Forrester, A., Sobester, A., & Keane, A. (2008). Engineering design via surrogate modelling:
a practical guide. John Wiley & Sons.
